teaching.concept: An H3 marks a heading at depth 3.
teaching.howTo: Start the line with exactly 3 hash marks, then a space.
teaching.example: ### After dinner

candidates.title: H3 heading
candidates.prompt: Write one H3 heading in Markdown.
candidates.target: ### Bring along

matchChecks.feedback: Add a space after the 3 hash marks.
Start the H3 with `### `.
Write exactly one H3 heading and no other blocks.

editorialChecks.review: Keep this focused heading plain unless emphasis is useful.

hints: Begin at the start of a line with ###.
Put one space after ###.
Example: `### After dinner`

teaching.concept: A short document uses heading depth to show how its sections fit together.
teaching.howTo: Rebuild the visible heading hierarchy and block order, using your own wording if you like.
teaching.example:
# Rainy walk

## Bring

Set these by the door.

- Raincoat
- Dry socks

candidates.title: Rebuild a sectioned checklist
candidates.prompt: Rebuild the rendered document as Markdown. Your wording may differ, but keep the same Markdown shape.
candidates.target:
# Bake sale

## Table supplies

Set these items beside the treats.

- Price cards
- Paper bags
- Small change

matchChecks.feedback: Keep heading levels in order without skipping a depth.
Keep the same heading, paragraph, and list or quote order shown in the Goal.
Finish with an unordered list of at least two nonempty items.

editorialChecks.review: Keep this focused heading plain unless emphasis is useful.

hints: Start with H1 and add the H2 section shown in the Goal.
Keep the paragraph after H2.
Finish with at least two bullet items.
